description:
compile_fn maintains two parallel HashMaps for variable state:
  var_map: HashMap<u32, ir::Value>    -- the Cranelift SSA value
  var_reprs: HashMap<u32, ArgRepr>    -- the repr (absent = Tagged by convention)

These are always updated together. The implicit-absence-means-Tagged convention in var_reprs
is fragile. Combine into one map with an explicit repr per entry.

ADD (before compile_fn, or alongside ArgRepr):
  #[derive(Clone, Copy)]
  struct VarBinding {
      value: ir::Value,
      repr: ArgRepr,
  }

IN compile_fn: replace both declarations (lines 3720-3721) with:
  let mut var_env: HashMap<u32, VarBinding> = HashMap::new();

UPDATE all insert sites (currently: match on repr, insert into var_reprs if non-Tagged, insert into var_map always):
  -- old pattern:
     match repr { ArgRepr::RawInt => { var_reprs.insert(p.0, ArgRepr::RawInt); } ... Tagged => {} }
     var_map.insert(p.0, val);
  -- new pattern (one line):
     var_env.insert(p.0, VarBinding { value: val, repr });

UPDATE all lookup sites:
  *var_map.get(&v).expect(...)  ->  var_env.get(&v).expect(...).value
  var_reprs.get(&v).copied().unwrap_or(ArgRepr::Tagged)  ->  var_env.get(&v).map_or(ArgRepr::Tagged, |b| b.repr)

UPDATE retag pass (currently iterates var_reprs):
  iterate var_env, filter repr != Tagged

UPDATE lower_prim SIGNATURE: replace
  env: &HashMap<u32, ir::Value>,
  var_reprs: &HashMap<u32, ArgRepr>,
with:
  var_env: &HashMap<u32, VarBinding>,

UPDATE lower_prim CALL SITE in compile_fn: pass &var_env

UPDATE tagged_get, as_raw_f64, as_raw_i64: take &HashMap<u32, VarBinding> instead of two maps.

cargo build must produce zero warnings. All tests must pass.
title: fz-cg2.5 VarBinding: combine var_map + var_reprs into one map
